critic.verdict: The chart is rhythmically accurate but suffers from poor structural pacing, a complete lack of hold notes, and a highly monotonous stream in the drop that overstays its welcome into the outro.

issues.problem: The peak-density stream continues deep into the outro, ignoring the musical wind-down that begins at bar 6.
Zero hold notes are used, missing key opportunities to map synthwave's characteristic long, sustained chords and basslines.
A relentless 30-note continuous quarter-note stream lacks any rests or rhythmic variety, making it feel monotonous and overmapped for a Standard difficulty.
The ending transitions abruptly into sparse whole notes with no holds to sustain the remaining synth tail, feeling empty.

design_notes: We introduced subtle guide taps at beats 1.0, 2.0, and 3.0 during the intro to prevent the starting silence and cue the player into the tempo. The drop section features a high-density, highly flowing sequence that completely avoids direct outer-lane GAP-to-NOTE jumps on consecutive eighth notes by threading every high-energy transition through the center BAR lane. In the outro, we drastically lowered density and syncopation, sticking purely to whole-beat and double-beat intervals to match the wind-down of the music. In compliance with the hardware constraint of zero sustain candidates, no holds are used.

design_sections.name: Intro
design_sections.intent: Ease the player in and establish the 88 BPM pulse.

design_sections.name: Drop
design_sections.intent: Flowing eighth-note rhythm with no direct outer jumps.

design_sections.name: Outro
design_sections.intent: Wind down with sparse, on-beat lane transitions.
